context.game: Pico Echo - virtual pet game where player cares for Echo the cockatiel
context.role: Autonomous grooming behavior that Echo performs randomly during idle time (30% chance)
context.screen_position: Center at x=64px, y=60px, head turns to side during animation
adjacent_elements: feather_particle (spawns to left/right at x=50-78px, y=55-65px)
context.usage_scenario: Randomly triggered when Echo is in idle state with 30% probability. Shows natural bird behavior of grooming feathers. Feather particles drift sideways. Lasts approximately 1.7 seconds then returns to idle. No player interaction required.

visual_specification.shape: Cockatiel with head turned to side (profile view of head while body remains frontal). Beak touches wing/body area. Crest tilted to side following head rotation. One wing slightly raised.

animation.description: Frame 1: Head starts turning left. Frame 2: Head fully turned, beak approaches wing. Frame 3: Beak touches wing, preening motion. Frame 4: Head turns to right side, beak to right wing. Frame 5: Head returns to forward position. Total duration: 1.67 seconds.

visual_specification.details: Head rotates approximately 60-70 degrees to side (profile view). Beak is 3x2px in profile. One eye visible when head turned (2x2px). Crest tilts with head rotation. Wing raises 2-3px when being preened. Feather particles (2x2px, #FFA300) spawn at frames 3 and 4, drift horizontally 8-10px over 0.5 seconds.

relationships.shared_with: Same color palette and base size as all Echo states
relationships.differences_from: Unlike idle: head rotated to side profile, beak touches body, wing raised. Unlike sleeping: eyes open, active movement. Unlike happy: no crest raise, no swaying, different particle type (feathers not hearts).

explicit_negations: No full body rotation (only head turns)
